Welcome

This tutorial will take you through the basics of CasualOS and how to use it.

Click the help button any time you get stuck.

Click the next arrow when you're ready to move on.

Colors

Information about bots, like their color, is stored in "Tags." We'll learn more about them later.

You can put the name of a color or a hex code in the bot's tag.

For a full list of supported color names, click here.

NOTE: You should only use lowercase letters in your color names. The computer will not know what you mean if you type "Red" instead of "red"

Scale

Just like their color, the size of a bot is stored in a tag

The default size of a bot is 1.

Any number between 0 and 1 will be smaller. Any number larger than 1 will be bigger.

More Scales

Since bots exist in 3D space, they care about 3 directions: X, Y, and Z.

They can be scaled up or down in only these directions with the scaleX, scaleY, and scaleZ tags.

Forms

You can make a bot any shape you want with the #form tag.

However, there are only a few shapes built-in to CasualOS. If you want a bot to be a different shape, we will cover how to do that later.

The shapes that are already built-in are:

  • cube
  • sphere
  • frustum
  • helix
  • egg
  • hex
  • circle
  • keyboard

Rotation

Bots can be rotated around the X, Y, and Z axes.

To rotate a bot, you need to change its homeRotationX, homeRotationY, or homeRotationZ tag.

Look out, though! The bots don't measure angles in degrees.

Instead, they use Radians.
